purpose: "Vector Loom"  -- 2d canvas tkinter structure vector graphic graphics.  a data format for vector structures, a runtime for using them, and a editor for such structures.  I frequently love using visual information, and this means I need to be able to author such structures and help agentic coding AIs make use of them.

general-background.reason: Read this for high-level orientation to Lion's recurring concepts, systems, and vocabulary.

general-background-abbreviated.reason: Read this for an abbreviated high-level orientation to Lion's recurring concepts, systems, and vocabulary.

project-directory-system.reason: Read this for a brief overview of how Lion's projects and repositories are organized.

programming-guidelines.reason: These are the programming guidelines that you shall implement the program with.

json-document-writing.reason: Read this when writing JSON documents intended for Lion's systems or for LLM consumption.

json-document-writing-minimal.reason: A condensed version of the JSON documentation-writing guide; read this for the essentials when the full guide is more than you need.

softspec.reason: Read this when specifying data structures in JSON documents; SoftSpec is the preferred approach.

softspec-v3.reason: Read this when specifying data structures in JSON documents; SoftSpec is the preferred approach. (v3 fixes minor errors in v2, includes 'json_clean', and describes how to incorporate data-dictionary entries.)

lionscliapp.reason: The program you are implementing will be built with the lionscliapp framework. Read this so that you know how to use it.

machine-root.reason: Read this when the application needs to resolve named machine-local resources through Machine Root.

tkinter-conventions.reason: When you write tkinter user interfaces, follow these guidelines.

tkintertester-reference.reason: When you write Tkinter GUI tests, use tkintertester. Read this so that you understand the harness, its event-loop-native test model, and its rules.

tkintertester-lionscliapp.reason: When you combine tkintertester with lionscliapp, follow this integration pattern so the CLI lifecycle and Tk test lifecycle stay correctly separated.

tkvillage-manual-v1.reason: Explains how to use TkVillage, a lightweight procedural Tkinter host runtime.

tkvillage-api-v1.reason: Provide a full user-facing API reference for the current TkVillage kernel.

tkvillage-data-dictionary-v1.reason: Document the runtime data owned by src/tkvillage/app.py using SoftSpec v3, including structural schemas and data-dictionary annotations for runtime ownership, lifecycle, reset behavior, and serialization boundaries.

m1-core.reason: You are going to be handling M1 data. Read this so that you understand the core structure and behavior of M1 entities and aspects.

m1-transport.reason: You are going to be storing or exchanging M1 data. Read this so that you understand its transport representation.

m1-link-types.reason: Read this when choosing link types for relationships in M1 data.

m1-merging.reason: Read this when combining M1 transport units or reasoning about merge behavior.

m1-runtime.reason: Read this so that you know how to use the M1 runtime.

reducer-core-architecture.reason: The program will use the Reducer-Core Architecture. Read this to understand the architecture and its specific terms of use.

cira.reason: The program is likely to use, or may be required to use, the Coordinated Interactive Runtime Architecture (CIRA). Read this so that you know how to implement it.
